heading: Earned value management and technology readiness levels for life extension programs
label: 6136 Earned value management and technology readiness levels for life extension programs
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , War and National Defense, prior to repeal by .section 2538f of Title 50Pub. L. 119–60, § 3111(b)(1)

children.heading: .—Review of Contractor Earned Value Management Systems
children.label: a .—Review of Contractor Earned Value Management Systems

children.text: review and validate whether the earned value management systems of contractors of the Administration for life extension programs meet the earned value management national standard; and
conduct periodic surveillance reviews of such systems to ensure that such systems maintain compliance with that standard through program completion.
establish specific benchmarks for technology readiness levels of critical technologies for life extension programs at key decision points; and
ensure that critical technologies meet such benchmarks at such decision points.

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: c .—Applicability
children.text: This section shall apply to programs that, as of , have not entered phase 3 of the nuclear weapons acquisition process or phase 6.3 of a nuclear weapons life extension program.January 1, 2021

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: d .—Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “earned value management national standard” means the most recent version of the EIA-748 Earned Value Management System Standard published by the National Defense Industrial Association.
